title: UPI OC No 229 FY 2025 26 Implementation of ISD GSTIN (Input Service distributor) option in URCS back office system
text:
NeEP

NATIONAL FAYMENTS CORPORATION OF INDIA

NPCI/UPI/OC No. 229/2025-2026 12 November 2025
To,
All Members of Unified Payment Interface (UPI)

Subject: Implementation of ISD-GSTIN (Input Service distributor) option in URCS
back-office system.

Reference may be taken from the notification No. 16/2024-Central Tax dated 6t" August
2024, regarding the Input Service Distributor (ISD) which is effective from 18 April 2025.

In alignment with this requirement, NPCI has invited few member banks to assess the
scope and system enhancements required. Based on the feedback received, a solution
has been designed and presented during a workshop on 24! July 2025, which was
unanimously accepted by participating banks. The necessary developments have since
been completed, and the back-office system is now ready for deployment.

An ISD/GSTIN update option is be made available in the URCS back-office system,
allowing members to configure their preferred reporting structure for GSTIN or ISD GSTIN.
These updates will be reflected in the monthly GST reports accordingly.

Timeline for ISD-GSTIN Updates: ISD-GSTIN updates must be completed before 20th
of each month to ensure the changes are reflected in the same month’s GST reports
(Example: If the ISD is updated on 20° November 2025, the changes will be reflected in
the November GST reports, which will be generated in the first week of December 2025).

Note: All banks’ GSTIN details are already configured in the URCS system at the time of
onboarding the bank in URCS. Members are advised to use the new update option only if
opting for ISD. Otherwise, the existing GSTIN-based reporting will continue unchanged

Go-Live Date: The ISD-GSTIN update functionality will be available in URCS effective
17 November 2025.

Annexure Reference: -

e Annexure A — Detailed step-by-step procedure for updating ISD/GSTIN in the
back-office system, along with the turnaround time (TAT) for completion.

e Annexure B — Comprehensive user manual for updating ISD/GSTIN in the back-
office system which includes maker & checker process.

Please disseminate the information contained herein to the officials concerned.

Warm Regards,

SD/-
Giridhar GM
Chief — Customer Success

NATIONAL PAYMENTS CORPORATION OF INDIA

Annexure -A
Procedure for Updating ISD-GSTIN in the Back Office System

1. Login and Navigation

To begin, the user must log in to the back-office system. From the home page,
select the "Update ISD-GSTIN" option in the main menu. The system will display
the definition of ISD details and prompt for confirmation. Click “Click to Proceed”
to continue.

2. Selecting Report Type and Updating ISD

After proceeding, the system will present options with two types of report: Payable
and Receivable. Each option includes two dropdowns: GSTIN and ISD. The user
should select the appropriate values based on the requirement and submit the
request.

Once submitted, the request moves from the Maker Tray to the Checker Tray.
The checker is responsible for reviewing and verifying the details entered by the
maker. Upon approval, the system updates the ISD information and generates the
monthly GST reports accordingly.

3. Timeline for ISD-GSTIN Updates
A) ISD-GSTIN updates must be completed on or before the 20th of each month
to ensure the changes are reflected in the same month’s GST reports.

Example: If the ISD is updated on 20" November 2025, the changes will be
reflected in the November GST reports, which will be generated in the first
week of December 2025.

2

Updates made after the 20th will take effect from the following month.

Example: If the ISD is updated after 20% November 2025, the effect will be
applied starting GST reports of Dec 2025 which will be provide in the first
week of Jan 2026.

Note: ISD is applicable for all type of income and charges, i.e. Switching Fee,
Interchange Fee, PSP Fee and any other Fee as applicable.

“Click to Proceed” then select ISD/GSTIN> Fill the details in all the fields

(all fields are mandatory) > Click on Submit. The request will then move
from the maker tray to the checker tray.

B) Checker Process
i) Checker should login to the back-office system and choose “Update

ISD-GSTIN” > Back-office system will display a page with all the
details entered by the maker > Checker will be provided with two
options “Approve or Reject” > User has to click on Approve > back-
office system ask for confirmation “Are you sure you want to approve
this ISD/GSTIN” > Yes /No > User to click on Yes > back-office
system provides the acknowledgement as “Action completed

successfully”.
